content:
Can I assume this is an IMAP account? What do you see when you view the Trash folder on your email provider's web mail interface? Are the errant messages visible there? If so, delete them there and try syncing Thunderbird again. If the messages reappear locally but not on the server, the local folder index may have become corrupt. This may fix it:

- Right-click on the folder and choose "Properties", then click the Repair button. Then compact the folder and hopefully things will show as expected. If not, let us know and there are some slightly more drastic possibilities.
creator: rednoise

content:
If your goal is to replicate her complete email setup, including all accounts, messages, folders, contacts, calendars, etc, your best bet is to copy her complete Profile from the old computer to the new. You can use a flash drive as a transfer vehicle. This article describes how:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/moving-thunderbird-data-to-a-new-computer

content:
This is a copy&paste from an answer I gave to a similar question a while back (see https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1570858#answer-1805771 ) ...

=

I did a little internet searching, and I don't see any sign that GMX supports OAuth2, which would explain why it's not an available option for you. I did find these generic email settings that you can try. You may need to confirm this info with their tech support:

Incoming (IMAP) Server: imap.gmx.com Port: 993 Encrytion: SSL or encryption

Outgoing (SMTP) Server: mail.gmx.com Port: 587 Encryption: STARTTLS

Outgoing (SMTP) Server: mail.gmx.com Port: 465 Encryption: SSL/TLS

Please note: You can only use a TLS protocol if your email program uses TLS versions 1.2 or 1.3. For security reasons, we no longer support the older TLS versions 1.0 and 1.1.

Info: If a connection via port 993 is not possible, you can try port 143. Please note that this connection is not encrypted by default. You can achieve encryption via port 143 by activating STARTTLS in your email program (e.g. Thunderbird, Outlook or Apple Mail) and using a valid SSL/TLS certificate.

==

I got this from:

https://support.gmx.com/pop-imap/imap/server.html

content:
This Nextcloud guidance advises: ‘Tasks can be synchronized using CalDav (each task list is linked to an Nextcloud calendar, to sync it to your local client: Thunderbird, Evolution, KDE Kontact, iCal … - just add the calendar as a remote calendar in your client).’ https://apps.nextcloud.com/apps/tasks

The Calendar is added as a remote calendar, and is syncing fine, so still miffed ...
creator: roundbot

content:
Success.

I was not aware that I needed two calendars in TB, one for the Calendar syncing, and one for the Tasks (confusingly a calendar - for the tasks) - to resolve copy the private link in Nextclouds tasks page, in TB go to tasks tab, create new calendar, and use the copied private link as the URL, you will need to add your cloud user name. This video, although dated shows roughly what you need to do:

https://youtu.be/gq9ccBbGGXk

Syncing for tasks that have not ‘started’ seem to take a little longer than those tasks that are created as started to sync, weird, however it all seems to be working OK now

content:
If thunderbird is still on the win 11 machine, try this:

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation on the win 11 pc
  2. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  3. exit thunderbird
  4. you are now in the profile folder. copy ALL of it and save to external media **
  5. start thunderbird on the linux PC
  6. repeat steps 1 through 3 above
  7. delete the contents of the profile
  8. paste the contents of the profile you copied from other PC
  9. restart thunderbird
    • TIP: Following step 3 above, if external media cannot contain the full size of profile, you can delete the message folders for IMAP accounts (such as inbox, inbox.msf, sent, sent,msf) as thunderbird will automatically repopulate the folders on restart

content: This a response from the server, not from thunderbird. It generally indicates the server is having problems delivering messages. content: I don't think I understand what you're saying. In general, with POP Thunderbird can only see Inbox on the server. You can tell Thunderbird to keep messages in Inbox on the server when downloaded, or remove them from the server once downloaded. Any other folders which may exist on the server are invisible for Thunderbird when using POP. Wrt sent messages, a copy goes to the local Sent folder only. There is no copy of sent messages on the server. IMAP works fundamentally different, where Thunderbird mirrors everything what's on the server.

content:
Beh, anzitutto riporta esattamente il messaggio di errore che ricevi. Tieni conto che Thunderbird è un programma che funziona sul tuo PC, non è servizio remoto. Nessuno può sistemarti il problema a distanza, ma possiamo eventualmente aiutarti a risolverlo.

Prova inoltre a fornire il maggior numero possibile di informazioni del seguente elenco:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)
creator: @next
